content.full_text:
RAvI MEI-ITA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 27,
1995
Bill Gordon,
Treasurer
Deyna for Mayor
1243 Gene Autry Trail,
Suite
K
Palm Springs,
CA 92262
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-95-195
Dear Mr.
Gordon:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.h/
Since your letter concerns general
questions,
we consider your letter to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation l8329(c).2/
During our conversation on June 13,
1995,
you indicated that
Deyna Hodges was elected to the Palm Springs City Council in 1993.
She now wishes to use the funds remaining in the bank account
opened for the city council election for a mayoral election in
1995.
You also stated that there are no outstanding debts
associated with the city council election and the upcoming mayoral
election is not a special election.
Ms.
Hodges may transfer the funds from the bank account
currently held for city council to a bank account opened for the
mayoral election and she can continue to use the same committee.
In order to change the name of the committee,
Ms.
Hodges must file
an amended Form 410 Statement of Organization to indicate the
revised name within 10 days of the change.
An initial Form 501
Candidate Intention statement must be filed before Ms.
Hodges
receives or solicits cOntributions for the mayoral election and an
initial Form 502
Campaign Bank Account statement must be filed
within 10 days of opening the mayoral campaign bank account.
1/
Government
Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government
Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Mr.
Gordon
Our File No.
1-95-195
Page Two
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

She now wishes to use the funds remaining in the bank account opened for the city council election for a mayoral election in 1995. You also stated that there are no outstanding debts associated with the city council election and the upcoming mayoral election is not a special election. Ms. Hodges may transfer the funds from the bank account currently held for city council to a bank account opened for the mayoral election and she can continue to use the same committee. In order to change the name of the committee, Ms. Hodges must file an amended Form 410 Statement of Organization to indicate the revised name within 10 days of the change. An initial Form 501 Candidate Intention statement must be filed before Ms. Hodges receives or solicits cOntributions for the mayoral election and an initial Form 502 Campaign Bank Account statement must be filed within 10 days of opening the mayoral campaign bank account. 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, .q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660 Mr. Gordon Our File No. 1-95-195 Page Two If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. Sincerely, Dixie Howard Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division
